About: It is about a relationship between a man and a woman.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the football club Tottenham Hotspur.
It is associated with the 1980–81 FA Cup.
ConsistsOf: It consists of pub singalong, music-hall humour, boogie-woogie piano and pre-Beatles rock 'n' roll.
CreatedBy: It was created by Chas Hodges and Dave Peacock.
CreatedFor: It was created for the club when Tottenham were having a good run in the 1980–81 FA Cup.
HasA: It has a musical style labelled rockney.
It has eight Top 40 hit singles.
It has a major breakthrough being "Gertcha" in 1979.
It has a song "Rabbit" and "Ain't No Pleasing You" as biggest success in the early 1980s.
It has a first studio album in 18 years released in October 2013.
It has a song "Snooker Loopy" released as a single in May 1986.
It has a traditional Christmas show.
It has a song "Rabbit" performed in 2016 on Strictly Come Dancing.

It is characterized by being partly comic.
It is characterized by being strongly identified with London's blue collar and working class pub sing-song culture.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by pre-Beatles rock 'n' roll.
It was influenced by the success of "Ain't No Pleasing You".
